activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: She have visited Prague twice.

evaluator.answer: She has visited Prague twice

activities.explanation: La tercera persona singular requiere el auxiliar «has».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: We have saw this exhibition before.

evaluator.answer: We have seen this exhibition before

activities.explanation: Después de «have» necesitamos el participio «seen», no el pasado «saw».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Omar has went to the pharmacy.

evaluator.answer: Omar has gone to the pharmacy

activities.explanation: El participio de «go» es «gone».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Have you ever eat Japanese food?

evaluator.answer: Have you ever eaten Japanese food

activities.explanation: La pregunta necesita el participio irregular «eaten».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The courier have just arrived.

evaluator.answer: The courier has just arrived

activities.explanation: El sujeto singular «courier» requiere «has».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: I have never drove an electric van.

evaluator.answer: I have never driven an electric van

activities.explanation: El participio de «drive» es «driven», no «drove».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Has they finished the safety check?

evaluator.answer: Have they finished the safety check

activities.explanation: El sujeto plural «they» se combina con «have».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The replacement part just has arrived.

evaluator.answer: The replacement part has just arrived

activities.explanation: La posición neutral de «just» es entre el auxiliar «has» y el participio.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: We have already book the meeting room.

evaluator.answer: We have already booked the meeting room

activities.explanation: Después de «have already» necesitamos el participio «booked».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Leo hasn't wrote the summary yet.

evaluator.answer: Leo hasn't written the summary yet

activities.explanation: La negativa necesita el participio «written», no el pasado «wrote».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: This is first time Mina has travelled alone.

evaluator.answer: This is the first time Mina has travelled alone

activities.explanation: La expresión fija requiere el artículo: «the first time».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: It is the best documentary I have ever saw.

evaluator.answer: It is the best documentary I have ever seen

activities.explanation: Después de «have ever» usamos el participio «seen».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The workshop have received twelve bookings this week.

evaluator.answer: The workshop has received twelve bookings this week

activities.explanation: El sujeto singular «workshop» requiere «has received».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Nina has been to the pharmacy, so she isn't at home now.

evaluator.answer: Nina has gone to the pharmacy, so she isn't at home now

activities.explanation: «Has gone to» indica que Nina se marchó y todavía está fuera.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Nina has gone to Rome three times and returned after every trip.

evaluator.answer: Nina has been to Rome three times and returned after every trip

activities.explanation: «Has been to» describe visitas completadas; «has gone to» implicaría que sigue allí.

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Neither parcel have arrived yet.

evaluator.answer: Neither parcel has arrived yet

activities.explanation: «Neither parcel» es un sujeto singular y exige «has».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: One of the reports have disappeared from the folder.

evaluator.answer: One of the reports has disappeared from the folder

activities.explanation: El núcleo del sujeto es «one», por lo que corresponde «has disappeared».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The final shortlist has just announced on the website.

evaluator.answer: The final shortlist has just been announced on the website

activities.explanation: La pasiva requiere «has just been announced».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: How many applications the office has processed so far?

evaluator.answer: How many applications has the office processed so far

activities.explanation: En la pregunta, el auxiliar «has» debe preceder al sujeto «the office».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The engineers have already chose the safer material.

evaluator.answer: The engineers have already chosen the safer material

activities.explanation: El participio de «choose» es «chosen», no «chose».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The results has already changed our understanding of the problem.

evaluator.answer: The results have already changed our understanding of the problem

activities.explanation: El sujeto plural «results» exige «have already changed».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: This is the first time either of us have led an international project.

evaluator.answer: This is the first time either of us has led an international project

activities.explanation: «Either of us» funciona como singular y se combina con «has led».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: The researchers have never came across this pattern before.

evaluator.answer: The researchers have never come across this pattern before

activities.explanation: El participio de «come» mantiene la forma «come».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: Neither supplier has gave us a firm delivery date so far.

evaluator.answer: Neither supplier has given us a firm delivery date so far

activities.explanation: Después de «has» usamos el participio «given», no el pasado «gave».

activities.instructions: Rewrite the complete sentence, correcting the present perfect error.
activities.prompt: We have compared every option, but we hasn't reached a decision yet.

evaluator.answer: We have compared every option, but we haven't reached a decision yet

activities.explanation: El sujeto «we» requiere «haven't reached» en la segunda cláusula.
